questionText: Você quer sugerir ao seu amigo que assistam a um filme novo. Qual expressão usar?
correctAnswer: Why don't we catch a flick tonight?
options: Why don't we catch a flick tonight?
I'm forcing you to watch a movie
You must see this film immediately
The film is obligatory for you

questionText: Seu colega recomendou um livro excelente. Como você expressa gratidão pela sugestão?
correctAnswer: Thanks for the tip, I'll definitely check it out.
options: Thanks for the tip, I'll definitely check it out.
I am forced to read your suggestion
You made me read this book
This recommendation is compulsory

questionText: Na festa, você quer saber a opinião de alguém sobre o show que acabou de terminar. O que dizer?
correctAnswer: What did you make of the concert?
options: What did you make of the concert?
I think you enjoyed the concert
The concert was good for you
You must tell me about the concert

questionText: Você quer convidar um colega para uma exposição de arte. Como fazer o convite de forma informal?
correctAnswer: Would you fancy going to the art exhibition next weekend?
options: Would you fancy going to the art exhibition next weekend?
You have to attend the art exhibition
I demand you come to the exhibition
The exhibition requires your presence

questionText: Um amigo compartilhou fotos de suas férias. Como você elogia as fotos de forma genuína?
correctAnswer: Those pictures are absolutely stunning! Where exactly did you take them?
options: Those pictures are absolutely stunning! Where exactly did you take them?
I like your photos because they show places
Your photos exist and they are visible
The photographs contain visual elements

questionText: Você quer perguntar ao seu amigo qual é seu gênero de música favorito. Qual pergunta é apropriada?
correctAnswer: What kind of music are you into these days?
options: What kind of music are you into these days?
Music is something you listen
You listen to sound waves regularly
Tell me the audio frequencies you prefer

questionText: Você quer se desculpar por ter perdido uma apresentação importante do seu amigo. Como expressar arrependimento sincero?
correctAnswer: I'm really sorry I missed your performance. I would have loved to be there.
options: I'm really sorry I missed your performance. I would have loved to be there.
You performed and I was absent
The event happened without my attendance
Your show took place in my absence

questionText: Alguém o convida para um evento cultural. Você tem interesse, mas precisa verificar sua agenda. Como responder?
correctAnswer: That sounds interesting. Let me check my calendar and get back to you.
options: That sounds interesting. Let me check my calendar and get back to you.
You invite me and I accept immediately
The event is in my schedule necessarily
I must attend your cultural event

questionText: Você está em um museu com um amigo e quer expressar admiração por uma obra de arte particular. Qual frase usar?
correctAnswer: I find this sculpture really fascinating. The detail is incredible.
options: I find this sculpture really fascinating. The detail is incredible.
This sculpture exists in the museum
The artist made this object
Sculptures are made by artists

questionText: Você quer discordar educadamente da opinião de alguém sobre um filme. Como fazer isso sem ser rude?
correctAnswer: I see your point, but I didn't quite feel the same way about it.
options: I see your point, but I didn't quite feel the same way about it.
You are wrong about the film
Your opinion is incorrect
I disagree completely with you

questionText: Seu amigo teve um papel em uma peça de teatro. Como você o parabeniza de forma calorosa?
correctAnswer: Congratulations on the performance! You really pulled it off!
options: Congratulations on the performance! You really pulled it off!
You appeared in a theatrical production
Acting occurred in your participation
You were on stage at a specific time

questionText: Você está em um festival de música com amigos e quer organizar onde vocês irão. Qual frase usar?
correctAnswer: Shall we head over to the main stage? The band I wanted to see starts in ten minutes.
options: Shall we head over to the main stage? The band I wanted to see starts in ten minutes.
There is a stage and music there
Bands play at festivals sometimes
Sound is produced by musical instruments

questionText: Você quer descrever o seu filme favorito para um amigo de forma entusiasmada. Como começar?
correctAnswer: You've got to see this film I watched recently. It absolutely blew my mind!
options: You've got to see this film I watched recently. It absolutely blew my mind!
A film exists and I viewed it
Movies are watched by people
I consumed a cinematographic product

questionText: Em um evento social, você quer iniciar uma conversa sobre arte contemporânea. Como começar?
correctAnswer: Have you been to any art exhibitions lately? I'm really interested in contemporary work.
options: Have you been to any art exhibitions lately? I'm really interested in contemporary work.
Art exists in contemporary times
Exhibitions display artistic objects
Contemporary means modern or recent

questionText: Você quer oferecer para pagar o ingresso do cinema para um amigo. Qual expressão usar?
correctAnswer: It's on me. Let me get your ticket.
options: It's on me. Let me get your ticket.
I will purchase your ticket
The ticket belongs to you now
Money will be exchanged for admission

questionText: Alguém lhe pergunta se você gostou de um show. Você gostou muito. Como responder de forma entusiasta?
correctAnswer: Absolutely! It was one of the best concerts I've ever been to.
options: Absolutely! It was one of the best concerts I've ever been to.
The concert was a positive experience
I was present at an audio event
Sound waves reached my auditory organs

questionText: Você quer sugerir uma série de TV para alguém. Como fazer a sugestão?
correctAnswer: I think you'd really enjoy this series. It's got amazing plot twists.
options: I think you'd really enjoy this series. It's got amazing plot twists.
A series exists with plot elements
Television shows have narratives
Entertainment programs are broadcasted

questionText: Você está em um museu e quer perguntar ao guia sobre uma obra específica. Como fazer a pergunta respeitosamente?
correctAnswer: Could you tell us a bit more about the story behind this painting?
options: Could you tell us a bit more about the story behind this painting?
This painting has a narrative
Artists create visual representations
Paint is applied to canvas surfaces

questionText: Você quer expressar dúvida sobre se um livro novo vale a pena ser lido. O que dizer?
correctAnswer: Is it worth getting? I've heard mixed reviews about it.
options: Is it worth getting? I've heard mixed reviews about it.
Books contain written words
Paper has pages with text
Opinions vary about all things

questionText: Um amigo comentou que não entendeu o final de um filme. Como você oferece sua interpretação sem soar pretensioso?
correctAnswer: The way I interpreted it was... but I could be completely wrong.
options: The way I interpreted it was... but I could be completely wrong.
My meaning of the ending is correct
I understand films better than you
Your comprehension is inadequate

questionText: Você quer recomendar um gênero de música específico a alguém que nunca ouviu falar dele. Como apresentar de forma intrigante?
correctAnswer: You should give indie folk a try. It's got such a raw, authentic feel.
options: You should give indie folk a try. It's got such a raw, authentic feel.
Folk music originates from traditions
Genres are categories of music
Sound waves have different frequencies

questionText: Alguém critica um artista que você admira. Como você defende sua opinião de forma respeitosa?
correctAnswer: I get what you mean, but for me, their work really resonates on a deeper level.
options: I get what you mean, but for me, their work really resonates on a deeper level.
Your criticism is invalid
Artists are always worthy of praise
Sound waves cause emotional reactions

questionText: Você quer perguntar a um amigo sobre seu hobby cultural. Como fazer a pergunta de forma natural?
correctAnswer: What got you interested in photography in the first place?
options: What got you interested in photography in the first place?
Photography is your hobby necessarily
You must enjoy taking pictures
Interest in activities is mandatory

questionText: Você assistiu a uma palestra sobre história da arte. Como você comenta sobre a experiência?
correctAnswer: The talk really gave me a fresh perspective on Renaissance art.
options: The talk really gave me a fresh perspective on Renaissance art.
A lecture about art was delivered
Historical information was communicated verbally
Words about art history were spoken

questionText: Um colega o convida para um festival de culinária internacional. Como você expressa interesse em aprender sobre diferentes culturas?
correctAnswer: I'd love to go! I'm always keen to explore different cuisines and cultures.
options: I'd love to go! I'm always keen to explore different cuisines and cultures.
Food comes from different countries
Cooking methods vary by region
People eat different things everywhere

questionText: Você quer expressar que um livro não era tão bom quanto esperava, mas de forma delicada. Como dizer?
correctAnswer: It wasn't quite what I expected, to be honest, but it had its moments.
options: It wasn't quite what I expected, to be honest, but it had its moments.
The book failed completely
Your recommendation was incorrect
I disliked everything about it

questionText: Você está em um café com amigos e quer sugerir ir a uma apresentação de dança. Como propor isso naturalmente?
correctAnswer: There's a contemporary dance show next Friday. Fancy checking it out together?
options: There's a contemporary dance show next Friday. Fancy checking it out together?
Dance exists and is performed
People move their bodies rhythmically
Movement can be artistic in nature

questionText: Você quer saber mais detalhes sobre um ator/atriz em um filme que assistiu. Como fazer a pergunta?
correctAnswer: Do you know anything about the lead actress? Her performance was remarkable.
options: Do you know anything about the lead actress? Her performance was remarkable.
An actress exists in the film
Actors perform roles in movies
Performances occur during film production

questionText: Um amigo o convida para ir ao teatro. Você está hesitante, mas quer ser honesto. Como responder?
correctAnswer: I appreciate the invite, but theater's not really my thing. But thanks anyway!
options: I appreciate the invite, but theater's not really my thing. But thanks anyway!
You invited me and I refuse
Theater is not an activity
I must decline your invitation

questionText: Você quer compartilhar sua experiência de ter visto um documental muito impactante. Como descrever?
correctAnswer: I just watched this documentary that really opened my eyes to the issue.
options: I just watched this documentary that really opened my eyes to the issue.
A documentary is a filmed record
Cameras record real events
Images are projected on screens

questionText: Você quer convidar alguém para uma noite de jogos de tabuleiro na sua casa. Como fazer o convite casualmente?
correctAnswer: Why don't you come over this Saturday? We could have a board games night.
options: Why don't you come over this Saturday? We could have a board games night.
Games are played by people
Houses are locations where entertainment occurs
Invitations are social communications

questionText: Você quer expressar decepção com uma estreia de filme muito esperada, mas de forma não definitiva. O que dizer?
correctAnswer: I had such high hopes for it, but it kind of fell flat for me.
options: I had such high hopes for it, but it kind of fell flat for me.
The film disappointed everyone
Movies rarely meet expectations
Films are always disappointing

questionText: Alguém pergunta qual livro você está lendo atualmente. Como responder de forma informativa?
correctAnswer: I'm reading a memoir by a travel writer. It's quite engaging so far.
options: I'm reading a memoir by a travel writer. It's quite engaging so far.
Books contain written language
Writers produce literary works
Reading is a common activity

questionText: Você quer pedir a opinião de um amigo sobre qual série de TV começar. Como fazer a pergunta?
correctAnswer: Have you seen anything good lately? I'm looking for something to binge.
options: Have you seen anything good lately? I'm looking for something to binge.
Television shows are for watching
Series consist of multiple episodes
Entertainment programs exist everywhere

questionText: Você está em uma galeria de arte e quer fazer um comentário sobre a composição de uma obra ao seu acompanhante. Como fazer de forma articulada?
correctAnswer: Notice how the artist uses light and shadow to create depth. It's quite skillful.
options: Notice how the artist uses light and shadow to create depth. It's quite skillful.
The painting contains visual elements
Artists apply pigment to surfaces
Colors exist within the artwork

questionText: Um amigo o convida para uma noite de cinema, mas você tem preocupações sobre saudade de alguém. Como expressar isso de forma sutil?
correctAnswer: I'd like to, but I've been feeling a bit blue lately, you know?
options: I'd like to, but I've been feeling a bit blue lately, you know?
I am sad and cannot go
You must understand my emotions
Emotional states prevent activities

questionText: Você quer critique construtiva de um amigo sobre uma apresentação musical que fez. Como pedir feedback de forma aberta?
correctAnswer: I'd really appreciate your honest feedback. What did you think of my performance?
options: I'd really appreciate your honest feedback. What did you think of my performance?
Tell me if I was good
Evaluate my musical abilities
Provide an assessment of my actions

questionText: Você está em um debate sobre a melhor forma de apreciar arte contemporânea. Como expressar uma opinião nuançada?
correctAnswer: I think the key is to approach it without preconceived notions and let it speak to you.
options: I think the key is to approach it without preconceived notions and let it speak to you.
Art communicates through visual means
Appreciation requires effort
Contemporary work is difficult to understand

questionText: Um colega questiona sua preferência por um gênero de música considerado 'pouco sofisticado'. Como defender sua posição?
correctAnswer: Music appreciation isn't about being pretentious. What matters is how it makes you feel.
options: Music appreciation isn't about being pretentious. What matters is how it makes you feel.
All music is equally valuable
Genres determine quality
Sophistication is the measure of art

questionText: Você quer explicar por que um filme antigo ainda é relevante nos dias atuais. Como fazer essa argumentação?
correctAnswer: Despite being decades old, it still resonates because it addresses timeless human themes.
options: Despite being decades old, it still resonates because it addresses timeless human themes.
Old films are less important
Modern films are always better
Time determines film quality

questionText: Um amigo não compreende sua paixão por um hobby cultural que você pratica. Como explicar sua motivação?
correctAnswer: It gives me a creative outlet and helps me express things I can't put into words.
options: It gives me a creative outlet and helps me express things I can't put into words.
I do it because I enjoy it
Hobbies are activities people do
Expression occurs through various means

questionText: Você está em uma discussão sobre a influência da tecnologia na forma como consumimos entretenimento. Como contribuir com uma perspectiva equilibrada?
correctAnswer: Technology has made entertainment more accessible, but sometimes I miss the experience of going out.
options: Technology has made entertainment more accessible, but sometimes I miss the experience of going out.
Technology changes everything completely
Entertainment is better now than before
Accessibility eliminates all problems

questionText: Alguém critica a indústria cinematográfica por falta de originalidade. Como você oferece uma visão mais matizada?
correctAnswer: While there's a lot of formulaic content, there are still filmmakers pushing creative boundaries.
options: While there's a lot of formulaic content, there are still filmmakers pushing creative boundaries.
All movies are the same
The film industry is completely broken
Creativity no longer exists in cinema

questionText: Você quer discutir como literatura pode funcionar como forma de escapismo. Como iniciar essa conversa?
correctAnswer: I think books offer a unique way to disconnect from reality and immerse ourselves in another world.
options: I think books offer a unique way to disconnect from reality and immerse ourselves in another world.
Books contain stories
Reading is a leisure activity
Imagination is used during reading

questionText: Um amigo está considerando abandonar seu hobby artístico. Como você oferece encorajamento genuíno?
correctAnswer: Don't give up on it yet. Your progress has been amazing, and it clearly brings you joy.
options: Don't give up on it yet. Your progress has been amazing, and it clearly brings you joy.
You should continue for external reasons
Hobbies are obligations
Joy is not a valid reason for activities

questionText: Você quer explicar a alguém por que um certo artista ou banda é subestimado(a). Como defender essa opinião?
correctAnswer: People tend to overlook their work, but if you really listen, you'll see the complexity in their artistry.
options: People tend to overlook their work, but if you really listen, you'll see the complexity in their artistry.
The artist is unknown
Everyone underestimates them
Their work is simple

questionText: Você está em um evento de lançamento de livro e quer fazer uma pergunta inteligente ao autor. Como formular?
correctAnswer: How did you go about researching this particular theme? It seems quite thoroughly explored.
options: How did you go about researching this particular theme? It seems quite thoroughly explored.
Books are written by authors
Research involves gathering information
Themes appear in literature

questionText: Alguém critica você por assistir muita TV. Como você responde defensivamente, mas de forma madura?
correctAnswer: I know some people see it as unproductive, but I find it relaxing and it's part of how I unwind.
options: I know some people see it as unproductive, but I find it relaxing and it's part of how I unwind.
Television is always a waste of time
You shouldn't judge my choices
Relaxation is not valuable

questionText: Você quer iniciar uma conversa profunda sobre como a arte molda percepções culturais. Como começar?
correctAnswer: Do you think art has the power to shift how we perceive cultural issues?
options: Do you think art has the power to shift how we perceive cultural issues?
Art is just for entertainment
Culture determines art completely
Perception is unchangeable

questionText: Um colega o convida para uma ópera, mas você nunca foi. Como expressar interesse apesar de suas dúvidas iniciais?
correctAnswer: I've never been to an opera before, but I'm willing to give it a shot. It might surprise me.
options: I've never been to an opera before, but I'm willing to give it a shot. It might surprise me.
Opera is boring
I have no interest in new experiences
Opera is not for people like me
